solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int maxLengthBetweenEqualCharacters(string s) {
        int maxLen = -1;
        int charIndex[26] = {0}; // To store the first occurrence index of each character

        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); ++i) {
            int idx = s[i] - 'a';
            if (charIndex[idx] != 0) {
                int len = i - charIndex[idx] - 1;
                maxLen = max(maxLen, len);
            } else {
                charIndex[idx] = i + 1; // Store i+1 to avoid -1 check for first occurrence
            }
        }

        return maxLen;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses an array to store the first occurrence of each character. For each character, if it has been seen before, the distance between the current index and the first index is calculated and compared to find the maximum. This approach ensures linear time complexity and constant space usage.>
